---
title: Estimation
description: Estimate unknown quantities from data.
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning Objectives

- Interpret point estimates.
- Interpret confidence intervals.
- Understand uncertainty in estimates.
:::

An estimate uses sample data to approximate an unknown population quantity.

::: {.key-idea}
A confidence interval communicates both an estimate and uncertainty.
:::

::: {.practice-box}
1. What is a point estimate?
2. Why is an interval usually more informative?
3. What happens to uncertainty with larger samples?
:::

## Solutions

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 1

Answer: a single-number estimate of an unknown quantity.

For example, a sample mean can estimate a population mean.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 2

Answer: an interval communicates uncertainty as well as the estimate.

A single number hides how precise or uncertain the estimate is.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 3

Answer: uncertainty usually decreases when the sample is larger, assuming the
sample is collected well.
:::
